description: The 'Garden of France' stretches 1,000km along the Loire River. Its cool climate produces the world's benchmark Sauvignon Blanc (Sancerre, Pouilly-Fumé), ethereal Chenin Blanc (Vouvray, Savennières) and age-worthy Cabernet Franc (Chinon, Bourgueil).

mythology.name: Bacchus
mythology.note: Roman viticulture along the Loire dates to the 1st century CE, documented by the poet Ausonius who praised the region's wines.

appellations.name: Sancerre
appellations.description: Perched on limestone hills above the Loire, Sancerre is the world's most precise expression of Sauvignon Blanc — flinty, mineral, aristocratic. Its Kimmeridgian limestone and silex soils give the wine its unmistakable gunflint character.
appellations.climate: Cool continental — cold winters, warm summers. Frost risk in spring.

tastingProfile.style: Flinty, mineral, aristocratic Sauvignon Blanc

appellations.name: Pouilly-Fumé
appellations.description: Directly across the Loire from Sancerre, Pouilly-Fumé grows exclusively Sauvignon Blanc on soils rich in silex (flint). The 'fumé' refers to the smoky, gunpowder character the silex imparts. Richer and rounder than Sancerre, yet equally mineral.
appellations.climate: Cool continental with Loire moderation.

tastingProfile.style: Smoky, flinty, richer-textured than Sancerre

appellations.name: Vouvray
appellations.description: Vouvray produces Chenin Blanc in every style from bone-dry to richly sweet — and some of the world's greatest sparkling wines. Its tufa limestone caves provide perfect ageing conditions. Dry Vouvrays can age 50+ years, developing extraordinary honeyed complexity.
appellations.climate: Oceanic — mild, maritime-influenced, ideal for Chenin's slow ripening.

tastingProfile.style: Electric Chenin Blanc — dry to luscious, ages magnificently

appellations.name: Chinon
appellations.description: The Loire's greatest red wine appellation. Cabernet Franc on gravel and tufa soils produces wines of elegant pencil-lead and raspberry character. Rabelais called Chinon 'the divine bottle'. With age, the best become hauntingly complex.
appellations.climate: Oceanic — cool, mild; Cabernet Franc ripens slowly and perfectly.

tastingProfile.style: Loire's finest red — elegant, pencil-lead Cabernet Franc

appellations.name: Muscadet Sèvre-et-Maine
appellations.description: The Loire's Atlantic gateway. Muscadet sur lie (aged on lees) produces dry, saline whites of surprising depth at remarkable prices. The best Muscadets — aged 24–36 months on lees — rival fine Chablis in mineral intensity.
appellations.climate: Atlantic maritime — mild, wet, strongly influenced by the ocean.

tastingProfile.style: Bone-dry, saline, lees-aged Atlantic white

description: The undisputed capital of fine wine. Over 7,000 châteaux, a millennium of winemaking, and the world's most influential classification system.

mythology.name: Bacchus
mythology.note: Roman amphorae bearing Bacchus imagery have been found across the Gironde region.

mythology.name: Dionysus
mythology.note: Greek traders brought Dionysian rites to southern Gaul from the 6th century BCE.

appellations.name: Pauillac
appellations.description: Pauillac contains three of Bordeaux's five Premier Crus — Lafite Rothschild, Latour and Mouton Rothschild. Its deep gravel terraces on the Médoc's left bank produce Cabernet Sauvignon of unrivalled structure, power and longevity.
appellations.climate: Maritime — Atlantic moderation, Gironde estuary influence.

tastingProfile.style: Supremely structured — the most powerful of the Médoc

appellations.name: Margaux
appellations.description: Margaux is the most perfumed and feminine of the Médoc appellations. Its fine gravel soils produce wines of extraordinary floral elegance, with violets and cassis intertwined with silky tannins. Château Margaux is one of wine's immortal names.
appellations.climate: Maritime with excellent drainage on gravel terraces.

tastingProfile.style: The most feminine of the Médoc — perfumed, silky, aristocratic

appellations.name: Saint-Émilion
appellations.description: The right bank jewel of Bordeaux. Merlot dominates on Saint-Émilion's limestone plateau and clay slopes, producing wines of sumptuous texture and plummy richness. The UNESCO-listed town is one of France's most beautiful medieval villages.
appellations.climate: Maritime, slightly warmer than the Médoc; clay retains moisture.

tastingProfile.style: Plush, Merlot-driven right bank opulence

appellations.name: Sauternes
appellations.description: The world's greatest sweet wine appellation. Morning mists from the Ciron river create perfect conditions for Botrytis cinerea (noble rot), which concentrates the Sémillon grapes to nectar. Château d'Yquem is wine's greatest sweet wine estate.
appellations.climate: Maritime with morning mists from the Ciron river — ideal botrytis conditions.

tastingProfile.style: Liquid gold — the world's most complex sweet wine

appellations.name: Graves / Pessac-Léognan
appellations.description: Bordeaux's oldest appellation, now split between Graves and the superior Pessac-Léognan. The only Bordeaux appellation that classifies both red and white wines. Château Haut-Brion, the only non-Médoc property in the 1855 Classification, sits here.
appellations.climate: Maritime, urban heat island effect around Pessac.

tastingProfile.style: Earthy, tobacco-driven — the most terroir-expressive of Bordeaux

description: Where terroir is religion. Burgundy's fragmented plots produce the world's most coveted Pinot Noir and Chardonnay.

mythology.name: Bacchus
mythology.note: Autun, the Roman capital of Burgundy, hosted one of Gaul's largest Bacchus cults.

mythology.name: Cernunnos
mythology.note: Pre-Roman harvest deity worshipped in Burgundy before Roman rites arrived.

appellations.name: Gevrey-Chambertin
appellations.description: Napoleon's favourite wine and Burgundy's most famous village. Gevrey contains 9 Grand Crus including the supreme Chambertin. The wines are the most powerful and structured of the Côte de Nuits, combining dark fruit with iron-fisted tannins.
appellations.climate: Continental — cold winters, warm summers. East-facing slopes for morning sun.

tastingProfile.style: The most powerful of Burgundy — iron fist in velvet glove

appellations.name: Vosne-Romanée
appellations.description: The most celebrated wine village on earth. The 1.8-hectare La Romanée-Conti Grand Cru, sole property of Domaine de la Romanée-Conti, produces perhaps the world's most expensive wine. Its neighbour La Tâche, Richebourg and Grands-Échézeaux are hardly less extraordinary.
appellations.climate: Continental, with the Vougeot château providing some wind protection.

tastingProfile.style: Transcendent — perfume, silk and eternal complexity

appellations.name: Meursault
appellations.description: The world's greatest village for white Burgundy. Meursault produces Chardonnay of incomparable buttery richness and hazelnut depth. No Grand Crus but its 1ers crus — Perrières, Charmes, Genevrières — are among Burgundy's most sought-after whites.
appellations.climate: Continental — south and east facing for perfect Chardonnay exposure.

tastingProfile.style: Buttery, nutty, richly textured white Burgundy

appellations.name: Chablis
appellations.description: The purest, most mineral expression of Chardonnay. Chablis' Kimmeridgian limestone — literally ancient seabed packed with oyster fossils — gives the wine a saline, oyster-shell character found nowhere else. Grand Cru Chablis is one of Burgundy's most underrated wines.
appellations.climate: Cold continental — the most northerly and coldest of Burgundy's sub-regions.

tastingProfile.style: The purest Chardonnay — steely, saline, no compromise

description: Since the 1976 Judgement of Paris, Napa has been the New World's benchmark for Cabernet Sauvignon — opulent, powerful, built to age.

mythology.name: Coyote Spirit
mythology.note: The Wappo people inhabited Napa Valley for thousands of years before European settlement.

appellations.name: Rutherford
appellations.description: Rutherford is where 'Rutherford dust' was born — a silky, almost earthy mid-palate texture unique to its benchland gravel soils. Some of Napa's oldest and most respected estates sit here, producing Cabernet of extraordinary finesse.
appellations.climate: Mediterranean — warm days, cold nights, fog from San Pablo Bay.

tastingProfile.style: The epitome of Napa — dusty, plush, eternally complex

appellations.name: Oakville
appellations.description: Oakville is arguably Napa's finest AVA — home to Opus One and Screaming Eagle. Its well-drained benchland soils and optimal sun exposure produce Cabernet of extraordinary balance between power and finesse.
appellations.climate: Mediterranean — warm, protected from coast. Classic Napa fog-free ripening.

tastingProfile.style: Napa's summit — power and finesse in perfect balance

appellations.name: Stags Leap District
appellations.description: The AVA that put California on the world map. In the 1976 Paris Tasting, Stag's Leap Wine Cellars' S.L.V. Cabernet beat Mouton Rothschild and Haut-Brion. The volcanic palisades retain heat and protect from wind, yielding silky, plush Cabernet.
appellations.climate: Slightly cooler south Napa — volcanic palisades create a heat trap.

tastingProfile.style: Silky, approachable — Napa's most elegant Cabernet

appellations.name: Santa Barbara County
appellations.description: Santa Barbara's transverse mountain ranges channel cold Pacific air inland, creating one of California's most exciting cool-climate regions. Made famous by the film Sideways, its Sta. Rita Hills AVA produces Pinot Noir that rivals Burgundy in elegance. Happy Canyon yields exceptional Bordeaux varietals.
appellations.climate: Cool maritime — transverse valleys funnel Pacific fog and wind inland. Extreme diurnal temperature variation.

tastingProfile.style: Cool-climate California — Burgundian elegance meets Pacific freshness

description: At Andean altitude, Malbec found its spiritual home. Mendoza's dry desert air, intense UV and cold nights produce wines of extraordinary depth.

mythology.name: Pachamama
mythology.note: Still honoured at harvest start with offerings of food, coca and chicha.

appellations.name: Luján de Cuyo
appellations.description: Argentina's first and only DOC. Luján's alluvial soils and altitude (900–1,100m) produce Malbec of extraordinary structure and complexity. The DOC guarantees organic practices and maximum yields. Home to Argentina's most prestigious producers.
appellations.climate: High desert — arid, intense UV, extreme diurnal variation (18–22°C daily swing).

tastingProfile.style: Argentina's finest Malbec — structured, complex, built to age

appellations.name: Valle de Uco
appellations.description: Argentina's most exciting wine frontier. At 1,000–1,500m altitude in the Andean foothills, Valle de Uco's rocky soils and extreme cold nights produce wines of unparalleled mineral precision and freshness — a revelation compared to warmer valley floor wines.
appellations.climate: Extreme high desert — highest UV in South America, coldest nights, dramatic diurnal swings.

tastingProfile.style: High-altitude precision — mineral, fresh, Argentina's emerging finest

description: The Cape's answer to Bordeaux — sun-drenched mountain flanks producing richly textured reds and home to Pinotage, South Africa's unique grape.

mythology.name: Qamata
mythology.note: The Xhosa are the indigenous custodians of the Western Cape. Qamata governs life's cycles and harvests.

appellations.name: Helderberg
appellations.description: The Helderberg mountain ward is considered Stellenbosch's finest terroir for red wines. Its decomposed granite soils, cooling ocean breeze from False Bay, and altitude produce Cabernet and Shiraz of extraordinary elegance and structure.
appellations.climate: Mediterranean — hot dry summers, cool mountain breeze from False Bay.

tastingProfile.style: Cape's finest — fynbos-scented, structured mountain reds

appellations.name: Simonsberg-Stellenbosch
appellations.description: The Simonsberg mountain ward produces Stellenbosch's most powerful and concentrated reds. Kanonkop, arguably South Africa's most celebrated estate, sits here — producing Pinotage and Paul Sauer (Bordeaux blend) of world-class pedigree.
appellations.climate: Mediterranean — sheltered north-facing slopes with longer ripening season.

tastingProfile.style: South Africa's most powerful — Pinotage and Bordeaux blends

description: One of the world's oldest wine-producing regions. The Phoenicians invented wine commerce and the Bekaa Valley's soils have yielded wine for 5,000 years.

mythology.name: Bacchus
mythology.note: The Temple of Bacchus at Baalbek, constructed c.150 CE, is the world's best-preserved Roman temple — a monument to wine in Lebanon's wine heartland.

mythology.name: El
mythology.role: Father of the Gods, associated with wine
mythology.note: The Phoenicians were the ancient world's greatest wine traders, spreading viticulture from Canaan across the Mediterranean from 3000 BCE.

appellations.name: Ghazir / Bekaa (Château Musar)
appellations.description: Château Musar's vineyards span from the high Bekaa to the Ghazir coast. This legendary estate, founded by Gaston Hochar, continued production even during Lebanon's civil war — bottling 1984 in a besieged country. Musar Rouge is fermented using 19th-century methods and aged for years before release.
appellations.climate: High-altitude Mediterranean — 1,000m, no irrigation needed. Rarely a failed vintage.

tastingProfile.style: Ancient, exotic — the Phoenician inheritance, unchanged
